‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا قَضَيْتُ ‏ ‏رَجَزِي ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَالَ هَذَا قُلْتُ أَخِي قَالَهَا فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَرْحَمُهُ اللَّهُ فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهِ ‏ ‏إِنَّ نَاسًا ‏ ‏لَيَهَابُونَ ‏ ‏أَنْ يُصَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَيَقُولُونَ رَجُلٌ مَاتَ بِسِلَاحِهِ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَاتَ ‏ ‏جَاهِدًا ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ شِهَابٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ سَأَلْتُ ‏ ‏ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏فَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏مِثْلَ الَّذِي حَدَّثَنِي عَنْهُ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏غَيْرَ أَنَّ ‏ ‏ابْنَ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ قَالَ مَعَ ذَلِكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَهَابُونَ ‏ ‏الصَّلَاةَ عَلَيْهِ كَذَبُوا مَاتَ ‏ ‏جَاهِدًا ‏ ‏مُجَاهِدًا فَلَهُ أَجْرُهُ مَرَّتَيْنِ وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِإِصْبَعَيْهِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, he said: Ibn Jurayj informed us, from Ibn Shihāb, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿAbdillāh ibn Kaʿb ibn Mālik al-Anṣārī informed me, that Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ said: On the day of Khaybar, my brother fought fiercely alongside the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, but his own sword recoiled and killed him. The Companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ spoke about it and doubted concerning him — a man who died by his own weapon — they doubted some of his affair. Salamah said: When the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ returned from Khaybar, I said: O Messenger of Allāh, will you permit me to recite rajaz poetry for you? The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ gave him permission. ʿUmar said to him: Mind what you say. He said: So I recited: "By Allāh, were it not for Allāh we would not have been guided, nor given charity, nor prayed." The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "You have spoken the truth." [Then continuing:] "So send down tranquility upon us, and make firm our feet if we meet [the enemy]. The polytheists have transgressed against us." When I finished my rajaz, the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Who said this?" I said: My brother composed it. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "May Allāh have mercy on him." I said: O Messenger of Allāh, by Allāh, some people are reluctant to pray over him and they say: A man who died by his own weapon. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "He died striving as a mujāhid." Ibn Shihāb said: Then I asked the son of Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, and he narrated to me from his father something similar to what ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated, except that the son of Salamah said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ also said: "They are reluctant to pray over him? They lied. He died striving as a mujāhid, and he has his reward twice," and the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ gestured with his two fingers.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏عَمْرُو بْنُ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَسَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا ‏ ‏كُنَّا فِي ‏ ‏غَزَاةٍ ‏ ‏فَجَاءَنَا رَسُولُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏اسْتَمْتِعُوا ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Razzāq narrated to us, he said: Ibn Jurayj informed us, he said: ʿAmr ibn Dīnār informed me, from Ḥasan ibn Muḥammad ibn ʿAlī, from Jābir ibn ʿAbdillāh and Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ — a man from the Companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ — that they both said: We were on a military expedition when the messenger of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ came to us and said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ says: "Enjoy [the mutʿah marriage]."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏قُرَّانُ بْنُ تَمَّامٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ الْيَمَامِيِّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِيَاسِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجْتُ مَعَ ‏ ‏أَبِي بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏غَزَاةِ ‏ ‏هَوَازِنَ ‏ ‏فَنَفَّلَنِي ‏ ‏جَارِيَةً ‏ ‏فَاسْتَوْهَبَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَبَعَثَ بِهَا إِلَى ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏فَفَدَى بِهَا أُنَاسًا مِنْ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ‏
en: Qurrān ibn Tammām narrated to us, from ʿIkrimah al-Yamāmī, from Iyās ibn Salamah, from his father, who said: I went out with Abū Bakr on the expedition against Hawāzin, and he gave me a captive girl as spoils. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ asked for her, and I gave her to him. He sent her to Makkah and ransomed some Muslims with her.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَيَّ مُتَعَمِّدًا ‏ ‏فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ ‏ ‏مَقْعَدَهُ مِنْ النَّارِ ‏
en: Al-Ḍaḥḥāk ibn Makhlad narrated to us, he said: Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Whoever deliberately lies about me, let him take his seat in the Fire."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَرَ رَجُلًا مِنْ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏أَنْ ‏ ‏يُؤَذِّنَ ‏ ‏فِي النَّاسِ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ عَاشُورَاءَ ‏ ‏مَنْ كَانَ صَائِمًا فَلْيُتِمَّ صَوْمَهُ وَمَنْ كَانَ أَكَلَ فَلَا يَأْكُلْ شَيْئًا وَلِيُتِمَّ صَوْمَهُ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd — meaning Ibn Abī ʿUbayd — from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, that the Prophet ﷺ ordered a man from Aslam to announce among the people on the day of ʿĀshūrāʾ: Whoever has been fasting, let him complete his fast, and whoever has eaten, let him not eat anything [more] and let him complete his fast.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏اسْتَأْذَنَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏الْبَدْوِ ‏ ‏فَأَذِنَ لَهُ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, from Salamah, that he asked the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ for permission to live in the desert, and he granted him permission.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَعَ النَّاسِ فِي ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَعَدْتُ ‏ ‏مُتَنَحِّيًا ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا تَفَرَّقَ النَّاسُ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَا ‏ ‏ابْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏أَلَا ‏ ‏تُبَايِعُ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ قَدْ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ أَيْضًا ‏ ‏قُلْتُ عَلَامَ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُمْ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏عَلَى الْمَوْتِ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: I gave the pledge of allegiance to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ along with the people at al-Ḥudaybiyyah, then I sat apart. When the people dispersed from the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, he said: "O Ibn al-Akwaʿ, will you not give the pledge?" I said: I have already pledged, O Messenger of Allāh. He said: "Again." I said: Upon what did you [all] pledge? He said: "Upon death."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ جَالِسًا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأُتِيَ بِجِنَازَةٍ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ دَيْنٍ قَالُوا لَا قَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قَالُوا لَا قَالَ فَصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ أُتِيَ بِأُخْرَى فَقَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ دَيْنٍ قَالُوا لَا قَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قَالُوا نَعَمْ ثَلَاثَ دَنَانِيرَ قَالَ فَقَالَ بِإِصْبَعِهِ ثَلَاثَ كَيَّاتٍ قَالَ ثُمَّ أُتِيَ بِالثَّالِثَةِ فَقَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ دَيْنٍ قَالُوا نَعَمْ قَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قَالُوا لَا قَالَ صَلُّوا عَلَى صَاحِبِكُمْ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏عَلَيَّ دَيْنُهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ فَصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd — meaning Ibn Abī ʿUbayd — from Salamah, who said: I was sitting with the Prophet ﷺ when a funeral was brought. He said: "Did he leave any debt?" They said: No. He said: "Did he leave anything?" They said: No. So he prayed over him. Then another was brought, and he said: "Did he leave any debt?" They said: No. He said: "Did he leave anything?" They said: Yes, three dīnārs. He gestured with his finger [and said]: "Three burns." Then a third was brought, and he said: "Did he leave any debt?" They said: Yes. He said: "Did he leave anything?" They said: No. He said: "Pray over your companion." A man from the Anṣār said: His debt is upon me, O Messenger of Allāh. So he prayed over him.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏عَامِرٌ ‏ ‏رَجُلًا شَاعِرًا فَنَزَلَ ‏ ‏يَحْدُو ‏ ‏قَالَ وَيَقُولُ ‏ ‏اللَّهُمَّ لَوْلَا أَنْتَ مَا اهْتَدَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَلَا تَصَدَّقْنَا وَلَا صَلَّيْنَا ‏ ‏فَاغْفِرْ فِدًى لَكَ مَا أَتَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَثَبِّتْ الْأَقْدَامَ إِنْ لَاقَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَأَلْقِيَنْ ‏ ‏سَكِينَةً ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏إِنَّا إِذَا صِيحَ بِنَا أَتَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَبِالصِّيَاحِ ‏ ‏عَوَّلُوا ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ هَذَا ‏ ‏الْحَادِي ‏ ‏قَالُوا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَرْحَمُهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ وَجَبَتْ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَوْلَا ‏ ‏أَمْتَعْتَنَا ‏ ‏بِهِ قَالَ فَأُصِيبَ ذَهَبَ يَضْرِبُ رَجُلًا يَهُودِيًّا فَأَصَابَ ‏ ‏ذُبَابُ ‏ ‏السَّيْفِ عَيْنَ رُكْبَتِهِ فَقَالَ النَّاسُ ‏ ‏حَبِطَ ‏ ‏عَمَلُهُ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ قَالَ فَجِئْتُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ أَنْ قَدِمَ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ فِي ‏ ‏الْمَسْجِدِ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ يَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّ ‏ ‏عَامِرًا ‏ ‏حَبِطَ ‏ ‏عَمَلُهُ قَالَ وَمَنْ يَقُولُهُ قَالَ قُلْتُ رِجَالٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏مِنْهُمْ فُلَانٌ وَفُلَانٌ قَالَ كَذَبَ مَنْ قَالَهُ ‏ ‏إِنَّ لَهُ لَأَجْرَيْنِ بِإِصْبَعَيْهِ وَإِنَّهُ لَجَاهِدٌ مُجَاهِدٌ وَقَلَّ عَرَبِيٌّ مَا مَشَى بِهَا يُرِيدُكَ عَلَيْهِ ‏
en: Ḥammād narrated to us, from Yazīd, from Salamah, who said: ʿĀmir was a man who composed poetry. He dismounted and began chanting the ḥidāʾ [travel song], saying: "O Allāh, were it not for You we would not have been guided, nor given charity, nor prayed. So forgive us — may our souls be Your ransom — for what we have committed. And make firm our feet if we meet [the enemy]. And cast tranquility upon us. Indeed, when the battle cry is raised upon us, we come. And with the battle cry, they have relied upon us." The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Who is this chanter?" They said: Ibn al-Akwaʿ. He said: "May Allāh have mercy on him." A man said: It has become obligatory [martyrdom], O Messenger of Allāh — if only you had let us benefit from him longer. He said: Then he was struck. He went to strike a Jewish man, but the tip of his own sword hit the back of his knee and killed him. The people said: His deeds are nullified — he killed himself. He [Salamah] said: I came to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ after he had returned to Madīnah while he was in the mosque, and I said: O Messenger of Allāh, they claim that ʿĀmir's deeds are nullified. He said: "Who says that?" I said: Men from the Anṣār, among them so-and-so and so-and-so. He said: "Whoever said that has lied. He has two rewards" — with his two fingers — "and he is indeed a striver, a mujāhid. Rarely has an Arab walked upon it [the land] who excelled him."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏صَفْوَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَمَرَ مُنَادِيَهُ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ عَاشُورَاءَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ مَنْ كَانَ ‏ ‏اصْطَبَحَ ‏ ‏فَلْيُمْسِكْ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ ‏ ‏اصْطَبَحَ ‏ ‏فَلْيُتِمَّ صَوْمَهُ ‏
en: Ṣafwān ibn ʿĪsā narrated to us, he said: Yazīd — meaning Ibn Abī ʿUbayd — informed us, from Salamah, that the Prophet ﷺ ordered his caller on the day of ʿĀshūrāʾ [to announce]: Whoever has eaten in the morning, let him abstain [for the rest of the day], and whoever has not eaten, let him complete his fast.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَفْوَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَمَّا قَدِمْنَا ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏رَأَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏نِيرَانًا تُوقَدُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عَلَامَ تُوقَدُ هَذِهِ النِّيرَانُ قَالُوا عَلَى لُحُومِ الْحُمُرِ الْأَهْلِيَّةِ قَالَ كَسِّرُوا الْقُدُورَ ‏ ‏وَأَهْرِيقُوا ‏ ‏مَا فِيهَا قَالَ فَقَامَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ الْقَوْمِ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَنُهْرِيقُ ‏ ‏مَا فِيهَا وَنَغْسِلُهَا قَالَ أَوَذَاكَ ‏
en: Ṣafwān [said], from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, from Salamah, who said: When we arrived at Khaybar, the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ saw fires being lit and said: "What are these fires being lit for?" They said: For the meat of domestic donkeys. He said: "Break the pots and pour out what is in them." A man from the people stood and said: O Messenger of Allāh, shall we pour out what is in them and wash them? He said: "Or that [will suffice]."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مَكِّيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ أَخْبَرَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجْتُ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏ذَاهِبًا نَحْوَ ‏ ‏الْغَابَةِ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا كُنْتُ ‏ ‏بِثَنِيَّةِ الْغَابَةِ ‏ ‏لَقِيَنِي غُلَامٌ ‏ ‏لِعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ ‏ ‏وَيْحَكَ ‏ ‏مَا لَكَ قَالَ أُخِذَتْ ‏ ‏لِقَاحُ ‏ ‏رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ مَنْ أَخَذَهَا قَالَ ‏ ‏غَطَفَانُ ‏ ‏وفَزَارَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَصَرَخْتُ ثَلَاثَ صَرَخَاتٍ أَسْمَعَتْ مَنْ بَيْنَ ‏ ‏لَابَتَيْهَا ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏صَبَاحَاهْ يَا ‏ ‏صَبَاحَاهْ ثُمَّ انْدَفَعْتُ حَتَّى أَلْقَاهُمْ وَقَدْ أَخَذُوهَا قَالَ فَجَعَلْتُ أَرْمِيهِمْ وَأَقُولُ ‏ ‏أَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَالْيَوْمُ يَوْمٌ أَقْرَعُ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَاسْتَنْقَذْتُهَا مِنْهُمْ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَشْرَبُوا فَأَقْبَلْتُ بِهَا أَسُوقُهَا فَلَقِيَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ الْقَوْمَ عِطَاشٌ وَإِنِّي ‏ ‏أَعْجَلْتُهُمْ ‏ ‏قَبْلَ أَنْ يَشْرَبُوا فَاذْهَبْ فِي ‏ ‏أَثَرِهِمْ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏ابْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏مَلَكْتَ ‏ ‏فَأَسْجِحْ ‏ ‏إِنَّ الْقَوْمَ ‏ ‏يُقْرَوْنَ ‏ ‏فِي قَوْمِهِمْ ‏
en: Makkī ibn Ibrāhīm narrated to me, he said: Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who informed him, saying: I left Madīnah heading toward al-Ghābah. When I reached the pass of al-Ghābah, a servant of ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿAwf met me. I said: Woe to you, what is the matter? He said: The she-camels of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ have been taken! I said: Who took them? He said: Ghaṭafān and Fazārah. He said: So I shouted three cries that were heard throughout the two lava plains: "O the morning raid! O the morning raid!" Then I rushed until I caught up with them after they had taken the camels. I began shooting arrows at them, saying: "I am Ibn al-Akwaʿ, and today is a day of destruction!" He said: I rescued them before they could water them, and I drove them back. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ met me, and I said: O Messenger of Allāh, the people are thirsty and I overtook them before they could drink, so send after them. He said: "O Ibn al-Akwaʿ, you have gained mastery, so be gentle. The people are being hosted among their tribe."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَكِّيٌّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏أَثَرَ ‏ ‏ضَرْبَةٍ فِي سَاقِ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏مَا هَذِهِ الضَّرْبَةُ قَالَ هَذِهِ ضَرْبَةٌ أُصِبْتُهَا يَوْمَ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَوْمَ أُصِبْتُهَا قَالَ النَّاسُ أُصِيبَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَأُتِيَ بِي رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَنَفَثَ ‏ ‏فِيهِ ثَلَاثَ ‏ ‏نَفَثَاتٍ ‏ ‏فَمَا ‏ ‏اشْتَكَيْتُهَا ‏ ‏حَتَّى السَّاعَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَاتِمٌ يَعْنِي ابْنَ إِسْمَاعِيلَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏خَرَجْتُ فَذَكَرَ نَحْوَ حَدِيثِ ‏ ‏مَكِّيٍّ ‏ ‏إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏وَالْيَوْمُ يَوْمُ الرُّضَّعِ ‏ ‏وَزَادَ فِيهِ ‏ ‏وَأَرْدَفَنِي ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ ‏
en: Makkī narrated to us, he said: Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, he said: I saw the mark of a wound on Salamah's shin and said: O Abū Muslim, what is this wound? He said: This is a wound I received on the day of Khaybar. The day I was struck, the people said: Salamah has been struck! So I was brought to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, and he blew on it three times, and I never felt pain from it until this very hour. Ibrāhīm ibn Mahdī narrated to us, he said: Ḥātim — meaning Ibn Ismāʿīl — narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, who said: I heard Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ say: I went out — and he mentioned a narration similar to that of Makkī, except that he said: "And today is the day of the sucklings!" And he added: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ seated me behind him on his riding camel.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَكِّيٌّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ آتِي مَعَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏الْمَسْجِدَ فَيُصَلِّي مَعَ ‏ ‏الْأُسْطُوَانَةِ ‏ ‏الَّتِي عِنْدَ الْمُصْحَفِ فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏أَرَاكَ ‏ ‏تَتَحَرَّى ‏ ‏الصَّلَاةَ عِنْدَ هَذِهِ ‏ ‏الْأُسْطُوَانَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَإِنِّي رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَتَحَرَّى ‏ ‏الصَّلَاةَ عِنْدَهَا ‏
en: Makkī narrated to us, he said: Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, he said: I used to come with Salamah to the mosque, and he would pray by the pillar near the Muṣḥaf. I said: O Abū Muslim, I see you deliberately choosing to pray by this pillar. He said: I saw the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ deliberately choosing to pray by it.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عُمَرُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ الْيَمَامِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمُ ‏ ‏سَالَمَهَا اللَّهُ ‏ ‏وَغِفَارٌ ‏ ‏غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهَا أَمَا وَاللَّهِ مَا أَنَا قُلْتُهُ وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ قَالَهُ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Ṣamad narrated to us, he said: ʿUmar ibn Rāshid al-Yamāmī narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, from his father, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Aslam — may Allāh grant it peace. And Ghifār — may Allāh forgive it. Indeed, by Allāh, I did not say it, but Allāh said it."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَدِمْنَا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةَ ‏ ‏وَنَحْنُ أَرْبَعَ عَشْرَةَ مِائَةً وَعَلَيْهَا خَمْسُونَ شَاةً لَا ‏ ‏تُرْوِيهَا فَقَعَدَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏جَبَاهَا ‏ ‏فَإِمَّا ‏ ‏دَعَا ‏ ‏وَإِمَّا ‏ ‏بَسَقَ ‏ ‏فَجَاشَتْ ‏ ‏فَسَقَيْنَا وَاسْتَقَيْنَا قَالَ ثُمَّ إِنَّ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏دَعَا بِالْبَيْعَةِ فِي ‏ ‏أَصْلِ ‏ ‏الشَّجَرَةِ فَبَايَعْتُهُ أَوَّلَ النَّاسِ وَبَايَعَ وَبَايَعَ حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ فِي وَسَطٍ مِنْ النَّاسِ قَالَ يَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏بَايِعْنِي قَالَ قَدْ بَايَعْتُكَ فِي أَوَّلِ النَّاسِ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ وَأَيْضًا فَبَايِعْ وَرَآنِي أَعْزَلًا فَأَعْطَانِي ‏ ‏حَجَفَةً ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏دَرَقَةً ‏ ‏ثُمَّ بَايَعَ وَبَايَعَ حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ فِي آخِرِ النَّاسِ قَالَ أَلَا تُبَايِعُنِي قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَدْ بَايَعْتُ أَوَّلَ النَّاسِ وَأَوْسَطَهُمْ وَآخِرَهُمْ قَالَ وَأَيْضًا فَبَايِعْ فَبَايَعْتُهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَيْنَ ‏ ‏دَرَقَتُكَ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏حَجَفَتُكَ ‏ ‏الَّتِي أَعْطَيْتُكَ قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَقِيَنِي عَمِّي ‏ ‏عَامِرٌ ‏ ‏أَعْزَلًا فَأَعْطَيْتُهُ إِيَّاهَا قَالَ فَقَالَ إِنَّكَ كَالَّذِي قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ ‏ ‏ابْغِنِي ‏ ‏حَبِيبًا هُوَ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ نَفْسِي وَضَحِكَ ثُمَّ إِنَّ الْمُشْرِكِينَ رَاسَلُونَا الصُّلْحَ حَتَّى مَشَى بَعْضُنَا إِلَى بَعْضٍ قَالَ وَكُنْتُ ‏ ‏تَبِيعًا ‏ ‏لِطَلْحَةَ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَحُسُّ فَرَسَهُ ‏ ‏وَأَسْقِيهِ وَآكُلُ مِنْ طَعَامِهِ وَتَرَكْتُ أَهْلِي وَمَالِي مُهَاجِرًا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ فَلَمَّا اصْطَلَحْنَا نَحْنُ وَأَهْلُ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏وَاخْتَلَطَ بَعْضُنَا بِبَعْضٍ أَتَيْتُ الشَّجَرَةَ ‏ ‏فَكَسَحْتُ ‏ ‏شَوْكَهَا وَاضْطَجَعْتُ فِي ظِلِّهَا فَأَتَانِي أَرْبَعَةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏فَجَعَلُوا وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ يَقَعُونَ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَتَحَوَّلْتُ عَنْهُمْ إِلَى شَجَرَةٍ أُخْرَى وَعَلَّقُوا سِلَاحَهُمْ وَاضْطَجَعُوا فَبَيْنَمَا هُمْ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ نَادَى مُنَادٍ مِنْ أَسْفَلِ ‏ ‏الْوَادِي ‏ ‏يَا آلَ ‏ ‏الْمُهَاجِرِينَ ‏ ‏قُتِلَ ‏ ‏ابْنُ زُنَيْمٍ ‏ ‏فَاخْتَرَطْتُ ‏ ‏سَيْفِي فَشَدَدْتُ عَلَى الْأَرْبَعَةِ فَأَخَذْتُ سِلَاحَهُمْ فَجَعَلْتُهُ ‏ ‏ضِغْثًا ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قُلْتُ وَالَّذِي أَكْرَمَ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدًا ‏ ‏لَا يَرْفَعُ رَجُلٌ مِنْكُمْ رَأْسَهُ إِلَّا ضَرَبْتُ الَّذِي ‏ ‏يَعْنِي فِيهِ عَيْنَاهُ ‏ ‏فَجِئْتُ أَسُوقُهُمْ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَجَاءَ عَمِّي ‏ ‏عَامِرٌ ‏ ‏بِابْنِ مِكْرَزٍ ‏ ‏يَقُودُ بِهِ فَرَسَهُ يَقُودُ سَبْعِينَ حَتَّى وَقَفْنَاهُمْ فَنَظَرَ إِلَيْهِمْ فَقَالَ دَعُوهُمْ يَكُونُ لَهُمْ ‏ ‏بُدُوُّ ‏ ‏الْفُجُورِ وَعَفَا عَنْهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأُنْزِلَتْ ‏ { ‏وَهُوَ الَّذِي كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنْكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ ‏} ‏ثُمَّ رَجَعْنَا إِلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏فَنَزَلْنَا مَنْزِلًا يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏لَحْيُ جَمَلٍ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَغْفَرَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لِمَنْ ‏ ‏رَقِيَ ‏ ‏الْجَبَلَ فِي تِلْكَ اللَّيْلَةِ كَانَ ‏ ‏طَلِيعَةً ‏ ‏لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابِهِ ‏ ‏فَرَقِيتُ ‏ ‏تِلْكَ اللَّيْلَةِ مَرَّتَيْنِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ثَلَاثَةً ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَدِمْنَا ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏وَبَعَثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِظَهْرِهِ ‏ ‏مَعَ غُلَامِهِ ‏ ‏رَبَاحٍ ‏ ‏وَأَنَا مَعَهُ وَخَرَجْتُ بِفَرَسِ ‏ ‏طَلْحَةَ ‏ ‏أُنَدِّيهِ عَلَى ‏ ‏ظَهْرِهِ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحْنَا إِذَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ الْفَزَارِيُّ ‏ ‏قَدْ ‏ ‏أَغَارَ ‏ ‏عَلَى ‏ ‏ظَهْرِ ‏ ‏رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَاقَهُ ‏ ‏أَجْمَعَ ‏ ‏وَقَتَلَ رَاعِيَهُ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Ṣamad narrated to us, he said: ʿIkrimah narrated to us, he said: Iyās narrated to us, he said: my father narrated to me, saying: We arrived with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ at al-Ḥudaybiyyah, and we were fourteen hundred. There were fifty sheep at the well that could not quench them. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ sat at the edge of the well, and either he supplicated or he spat into it, and it gushed forth. We drank and drew water. Then the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ called for the pledge at the base of the tree. I pledged to him first among the people. He continued taking pledges until, when he was in the middle of the people, he said: "O Salamah, pledge to me." I said: I already pledged to you first among the people, O Messenger of Allāh. He said: "And again." So I pledged. He saw I was unarmed and gave me a shield — or a buckler. Then he continued taking pledges until, when he was among the last of the people, he said: "Will you not pledge to me?" I said: O Messenger of Allāh, I have already pledged first, in the middle, and last. He said: "And again." So I pledged again. Then he said: "Where is the buckler — or shield — I gave you?" I said: O Messenger of Allāh, my uncle ʿĀmir met me unarmed, so I gave it to him. He said: "You are like the one who said: 'O Allāh, find me a beloved who is dearer to me than myself,'" and he laughed. Then the polytheists sent us peace proposals until we mixed with one another. I was a servant of Ṭalḥah ibn ʿUbaydillāh — I groomed his horse, watered it, and ate from his food, having left my family and wealth as an emigrant to Allāh and His Messenger. When we and the people of Makkah made peace and mixed with each other, I came to the tree, cleared its thorns, and lay down in its shade. Four men from the people of Makkah came to me, and they — being polytheists — began insulting the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. So I moved away from them to another tree. They hung up their weapons and lay down. While they were thus, a caller cried from the bottom of the valley: "O Muhājirūn! Ibn Zunaym has been killed!" So I drew my sword and charged the four men, seized their weapons, and bundled them together. Then I said: By the One who honored Muḥammad, none of you shall raise his head without me striking what is between his eyes! I drove them to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, and my uncle ʿĀmir came with Ibn Mikraz, leading his horse, leading seventy [captives], until we stood them before him. He looked at them and said: "Let them go — let the beginning of treachery be theirs." The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ pardoned them, and the verse was revealed: {And it is He who restrained their hands from you and your hands from them} [48:24]. Then we returned to Madīnah and stopped at a place called Laḥy Jamal. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ sought forgiveness for whoever climbed the mountain that night as a scout for the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and his companions. I climbed it that night two or three times. Then we arrived in Madīnah, and the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ sent his riding animals with his servant Rabāḥ, and I was with him. I went out with Ṭalḥah's horse to graze it among the riding animals. When morning came, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿUyaynah al-Fazārī had raided the riding animals of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, driven them all away, and killed their herdsman.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏نَزَلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْزِلًا فَجَاءَ عَيْنٌ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابُهُ يَتَصَبَّحُونَ فَدَعَوْهُ إِلَى طَعَامِهِمْ فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ الرَّجُلُ رَكِبَ عَلَى ‏ ‏رَاحِلَتِهِ ‏ ‏وَذَهَبَ مُسْرِعًا لِيُنْذِرَ أَصْحَابَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَأَدْرَكْتُهُ ‏ ‏فَأَنَخْتُ ‏ ‏رَاحِلَتَهُ ‏ ‏وَضَرَبْتُ عُنُقَهُ ‏ ‏فَغَنَّمَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏سَلَبَهُ ‏
en: ʿAbdullāh ibn Yazīd narrated to us, he said: ʿIkrimah ibn ʿAmmār narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ stopped at a place, and a spy of the polytheists came while the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and his companions were having their morning meal. They invited him to eat with them. When the man finished, he mounted his riding camel and left quickly to warn his companions. Salamah said: I caught up with him, made his camel kneel, and struck his neck. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ gave me his spoils.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَطَّافُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ لِلنَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَكُونُ أَحْيَانًا فِي الصَّيْدِ ‏ ‏فَأُصَلِّي فِي قَمِيصِي فَقَالَ ‏ ‏زُرَّهُ ‏ ‏وَلَوْ لَمْ تَجِدْ إِلَّا شَوْكَةً ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Khālid narrated to us, he said: ʿAṭṭāf ibn Khālid narrated to us, from Mūsā ibn Ibrāhīm, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: I said to the Prophet ﷺ: Sometimes I am out hunting and I pray in just my shirt. He said: "Button it up, even if only with a thorn."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَيُّوبَ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِيَاسِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا حَضَرَتْ الصَّلَاةُ وَالْعَشَاءُ فَابْدَءُوا بِالْعَشَاءِ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Khālid narrated to us, from Ayyūb ibn ʿUtbah, from Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "When the prayer and the evening meal are both present, begin with the meal."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَطَّافٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏إِنِّي أَكُونُ فِي الصَّيْدِ ‏ ‏فَأُصَلِّي وَلَيْسَ عَلَيَّ إِلَّا قَمِيصٌ وَاحِدٌ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَزُرَّهُ ‏ ‏وَإِنْ لَمْ تَجِدْ إِلَّا شَوْكَةً ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, he said: ʿAṭṭāf narrated to us, from Mūsā ibn Ibrāhīm ibn Abī Rabīʿah, who said: I heard Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ say: I said: O Messenger of Allāh, I am sometimes out hunting and I pray with nothing on but a single shirt. He said: "Button it up, even if you can find nothing but a thorn."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏غَزَوْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏هَوَازِنَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَبَيْنَمَا نَحْنُ ‏ ‏نَتَضَحَّى ‏ ‏وَعَامَّتُنَا مُشَاةٌ فِينَا ضَعَفَةٌ إِذْ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى جَمَلٍ أَحْمَرَ فَانْتَزَعَ ‏ ‏طَلَقًا ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏حَقَبِهِ ‏ ‏فَقَيَّدَ بِهِ جَمَلَهُ رَجُلٌ شَابٌّ ثُمَّ جَاءَ يَتَغَدَّى مَعَ الْقَوْمِ فَلَمَّا رَأَى ضَعْفَهُمْ وَرِقَّةَ ‏ ‏ظَهْرِهِمْ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ إِلَى جَمَلِهِ فَأَطْلَقَهُ ثُمَّ ‏ ‏أَنَاخَهُ ‏ ‏فَقَعَدَ عَلَيْهِ فَخَرَجَ ‏ ‏يَرْكُضُ ‏ ‏وَتَبِعَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏صَحَابَةِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى نَاقَةٍ ‏ ‏وَرْقَاءَ ‏ ‏هِيَ أَمْثَلُ ‏ ‏ظَهْرِ ‏ ‏الْقَوْمِ فَأَتْبَعَهُ قَالَ وَخَرَجْتُ ‏ ‏أَعْدُو فَأَدْرَكْتُهُ وَرَأْسُ النَّاقَةِ عِنْدَ وَرِكِ الْجَمَلِ وَكُنْتُ عِنْدَ وَرِكِ النَّاقَةِ ثُمَّ تَقَدَّمْتُ حَتَّى كُنْتُ عِنْدَ وَرِكِ الْجَمَلِ ثُمَّ تَقَدَّمْتُ حَتَّى أَخَذْتُ ‏ ‏بِخِطَامِ ‏ ‏الْجَمَلِ ‏ ‏فَأَنَخْتُهُ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا وَضَعَ رُكْبَتَهُ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ‏ ‏اخْتَرَطْتُ ‏ ‏سَيْفِي فَأَضْرِبُ بِهِ رَأْسَهُ ‏ ‏فَنَدَرَ ‏ ‏فَجِئْتُ ‏ ‏بِرَاحِلَتِهِ ‏ ‏وَمَا عَلَيْهَا أَقُودُهُ فَاسْتَقْبَلَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مُقْبِلًا قَالَ مَنْ قَتَلَ الرَّجُلَ قَالُوا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏لَهُ ‏ ‏سَلَبُهُ ‏ ‏أَجْمَعُ ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, he said: ʿIkrimah narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to me, he said: my father narrated to me, saying: I went on the expedition against Hawāzin with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. While we were having our morning meal — most of us on foot, with weak ones among us — a man came on a red camel. He took a strap from its belly-band and hobbled his camel — a young man. Then he came to eat the morning meal with the people. When he saw their weakness and the poor condition of their mounts, he went to his camel, untied it, made it kneel, mounted it, and galloped off. A man from Aslam, one of the companions of the Prophet ﷺ, pursued him on a gray she-camel that was the best mount of the group. I went out running and caught up with him while the she-camel's head was at the camel's flank, and I was at the she-camel's flank. Then I moved ahead until I was at the camel's flank. Then I moved ahead until I seized the camel's halter and made it kneel. When its knee touched the ground, I drew my sword and struck his head — it flew off. I brought his riding camel and what was on it, leading it. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ met me coming toward him and said: "Who killed the man?" They said: Ibn al-Akwaʿ. He said: "All his spoils belong to him."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَقُولُ أَحَدٌ عَلَيَّ بَاطِلًا ‏ ‏أَوْ مَا لَمْ أَقُلْ ‏ ‏إِلَّا ‏ ‏تَبَوَّأَ ‏ ‏مَقْعَدَهُ مِنْ النَّارِ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, he said: Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Let no one say anything false about me — or what I did not say — except that he takes his seat in the Fire."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجْنَا إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ الْقَوْمِ أَيْ ‏ ‏عَامِرُ ‏ ‏لَوْ أَسْمَعْتَنَا مِنْ ‏ ‏هُنَيَّاتِكَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَنَزَلَ ‏ ‏يَحْدُو ‏ ‏بِهِمْ وَيَذْكُرُ ‏ ‏تَالَلَّهِ لَوْلَا اللَّهُ مَا اهْتَدَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَذَكَرَ شِعْرًا غَيْرَ هَذَا وَلَكِنْ لَمْ أَحْفَظْ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ هَذَا السَّائِقُ قَالُوا ‏ ‏عَامِرُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَرْحَمُهُ اللَّهُ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ الْقَوْمِ يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ لَوْلَا ‏ ‏مَتَّعْتَنَا ‏ ‏بِهِ فَلَمَّا ‏ ‏اصَّافَّ ‏ ‏الْقَوْمُ قَاتَلُوهُمْ فَأُصِيبَ ‏ ‏عَامِرُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏بِقَائِمِ سَيْفِ نَفْسِهِ فَمَاتَ فَلَمَّا أَمْسَوْا أَوْقَدُوا نَارًا كَثِيرَةً فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَا هَذِهِ النَّارُ عَلَى أَيِّ شَيْءٍ تُوقَدُ قَالُوا عَلَى حُمُرٍ إِنْسِيَّةٍ قَالَ ‏ ‏أَهْرِيقُوا ‏ ‏مَا فِيهَا وَكَسِّرُوهَا فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ أَلَا ‏ ‏نُهَرِيقُ ‏ ‏مَا فِيهَا وَنَغْسِلُهَا قَالَ أَوْ ذَاكَ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, he said: Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, who said: We went out with the Prophet ﷺ to Khaybar. A man from the group said: O ʿĀmir, let us hear some of your little songs. So he dismounted and began chanting the ḥidāʾ for them, reciting: "By Allāh, were it not for Allāh we would not have been guided..." — and he mentioned other poetry I did not memorize. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Who is this driver?" They said: ʿĀmir ibn al-Akwaʿ. He said: "May Allāh have mercy on him." A man from the group said: O Prophet of Allāh, if only you had let us benefit from him longer. When the two sides lined up and they fought, ʿĀmir ibn al-Akwaʿ was struck by the pommel of his own sword and died. When evening came, they lit many fires. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "What are these fires? What are they being lit for?" They said: For domestic donkeys. He said: "Pour out what is in them and break the pots." A man said: Shall we pour out what is in them and wash them? He said: "Or that."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏أَذِّنْ فِي قَوْمِكَ ‏ ‏أَوْ فِي النَّاسِ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ عَاشُورَاءَ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَكَلَ فَلْيَصُمْ بَقِيَّةَ يَوْمِهِ وَمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ أَكَلَ فَلْيَصُمْ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, he said: Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said to a man from Aslam: "Announce among your people — or among the people — on the day of ʿĀshūrāʾ: Whoever has eaten, let him fast the remainder of his day, and whoever has not eaten, let him fast."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأُتِيَ بِجِنَازَةٍ فَقَالُوا يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ صَلِّ عَلَيْهَا قَالَ ‏ ‏هَلْ تَرَكَ شَيْئًا قَالُوا لَا قَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنًا قَالُوا لَا فَصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ أُتِيَ بِجَنَازَةٍ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ دَيْنٍ قَالُوا لَا قَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قَالُوا ثَلَاثَ دَنَانِيرَ قَالَ ثَلَاثُ كَيَّاتٍ قَالَ فَأُتِيَ بِالثَّالِثَةِ فَقَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ دَيْنٍ قَالُوا نَعَمْ قَالَ هَلْ تَرَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قَالُوا لَا قَالَ صَلُّوا عَلَى صَاحِبِكُمْ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلَيَّ دَيْنُهُ فَصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Yazīd, he said: Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, who said: I was with the Prophet ﷺ when a funeral was brought. They said: O Prophet of Allāh, pray over him. He said: "Did he leave anything?" They said: No. He said: "Did he leave any debt?" They said: No. So he prayed over him. Then another funeral was brought after that. He said: "Did he leave any debt upon him?" They said: No. He said: "Did he leave anything?" They said: Three dīnārs. He said: "Three burns!" Then a third was brought. He said: "Did he leave any debt upon him?" They said: Yes. He said: "Did he leave anything?" They said: No. He said: "Pray over your companion." A man from the Anṣār — said to be Abū Qatādah — said: O Messenger of Allāh, his debt is upon me. So he prayed over him.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى قَوْمٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏وَهُمْ ‏ ‏يَتَنَاضَلُونَ ‏ ‏فِي السُّوقِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏ارْمُوا يَا ‏ ‏بَنِي إِسْمَاعِيلَ ‏ ‏فَإِنَّ أَبَاكُمْ كَانَ رَامِيًا ارْمُوا وَأَنَا مَعَ بَنِي فُلَانٍ لِأَحَدِ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ فَأَمْسَكُوا أَيْدِيَهُمْ فَقَالَ ارْمُوا قَالُوا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ ‏ ‏نَرْمِي وَأَنْتَ مَعَ بَنِي فُلَانٍ قَالَ ارْمُوا وَأَنَا مَعَكُمْ كُلُّكُمْ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd, he said: Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to me, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ came out upon some people of Aslam who were competing in archery in the marketplace. He said: "Shoot, O sons of Ismāʿīl, for your father was an archer! Shoot, and I am with the tribe of so-and-so" — one of the two teams. They withheld their hands [stopped shooting]. He said: "Shoot!" They said: O Messenger of Allāh, how can we shoot when you are with the tribe of so-and-so? He said: "Shoot, and I am with all of you."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّ ‏ ‏أَبَاهُ ‏ ‏أَخْبَرَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَّ رَجُلًا عَطَسَ عِنْدَ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ ثُمَّ عَطَسَ الثَّانِيَةَ ‏ ‏أَوْ الثَّالِثَةَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِنَّهُ مَزْكُومٌ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from ʿIkrimah ibn ʿAmmār, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah narrated to me, that his father informed him that a man sneezed in the presence of the Prophet ﷺ, and the Prophet ﷺ said to him: "May Allāh have mercy on you." Then he sneezed a second time — or a third — and the Prophet ﷺ said: "He has a cold."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏رَأَى ‏ ‏رَجُلًا ‏ ‏يَأْكُلُ بِشِمَالِهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏كُلْ بِيَمِينِكَ قَالَ لَا أَسْتَطِيعُ قَالَ لَا اسْتَطَعْتَ قَالَ فَمَا وَصَلَتْ إِلَى ‏ ‏فِيهِ بَعْدُ ‏
en: Yaḥyā ibn Saʿīd narrated to us, from ʿIkrimah, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah narrated to me, from his father, that the Prophet ﷺ saw a man eating with his left hand and said: "Eat with your right hand." He said: I cannot. He said: "May you never be able to!" And it [his hand] never reached his mouth after that.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَوْنٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عُمَيْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِيَاسِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ ‏ ‏عَيْنٌ ‏ ‏لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَلَمَّا طَعِمَ ‏ ‏انْسَلَّ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى الرَّجُلِ اقْتُلُوا قَالَ فَابْتَدَرَ الْقَوْمُ قَالَ وَكَانَ أَبِي ‏ ‏يَسْبِقُ ‏ ‏الْفَرَسَ شَدًّا قَالَ فَسَبَقَهُمْ إِلَيْهِ قَالَ فَأَخَذَ ‏ ‏بِزِمَامِ ‏ ‏نَاقَتِهِ ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏بِخِطَامِهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ ثُمَّ قَتَلَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏فَنَفَّلَهُ ‏ ‏رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏سَلَبَهُ ‏
en: Jaʿfar ibn ʿAwn narrated to us, he said: Abū ʿUmays narrated to us, from Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, from his father, who said: A spy of the polytheists came to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. When he had eaten, he slipped away. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said regarding the man: "Kill him!" The people rushed [after him]. My father used to outrun horses in sprinting. He outran them to him and seized the halter — or the rein — of his camel, then killed him. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ gave him his spoils.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏صَفْوَانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يُصَلِّي الْمَغْرِبَ سَاعَةَ تَغْرُبُ الشَّمْسُ إِذَا غَابَ حَاجِبُهَا ‏
en: Ṣafwān narrated to us, he said: Ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ used to pray Maghrib the moment the sun set, when its edge disappeared.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏صَفْوَانُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ ‏ ‏لِسَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَلَى أَيِّ شَيْءٍ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُمْ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَوْمَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَايَعْنَاهُ ‏ ‏عَلَى الْمَوْتِ ‏
en: Ṣafwān narrated to us, he said: Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, he said: I asked Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ: Upon what did you pledge allegiance to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ on the day of al-Ḥudaybiyyah? He said: We pledged allegiance to him upon death.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏شُعْبَةُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏الْحَسَنَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ ‏ ‏جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏وَسَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَا ‏ ‏خَرَجَ عَلَيْنَا مُنَادِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَنَادَى إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَدْ ‏ ‏أَذِنَ ‏ ‏لَكُمْ ‏ ‏فَاسْتَمْتِعُوا ‏ ‏يَعْنِي ‏ ‏مُتْعَةَ النِّسَاءِ ‏
en: Muḥammad ibn Jaʿfar narrated to us, he said: Shuʿbah narrated to us, from ʿAmr ibn Dīnār, who said: I heard al-Ḥasan ibn Muḥammad narrating from Jābir ibn ʿAbdillāh and Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: The caller of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ came out to us and called: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ has given you permission, so enjoy [the mutʿah] — meaning the temporary marriage of women.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زُهَيْرٍ ‏ ‏وَحَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي بُكَيْرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏زُهَيْرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ بْنِ خُصَيْفَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ أُسَافِرُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَمَا رَأَيْتُهُ صَلَّى بَعْدَ الْعَصْرِ وَلَا بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ قَطُّ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Zuhayr. And Yaḥyā ibn Abī Bukayr narrated to us, he said: Zuhayr ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Khuṣayfah, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: I used to travel with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, and I never saw him pray after ʿAṣr or after Fajr — ever.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏بَهْزُ بْنُ أَسَدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏غَزَوْنَا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏هَوَازِنَ ‏ ‏وَغَطَفَانَ ‏ ‏فَبَيْنَمَا نَحْنُ كَذَلِكَ إِذْ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى جَمَلٍ أَحْمَرَ فَانْتَزَعَ شَيْئًا مِنْ ‏ ‏حَقَبِ ‏ ‏الْبَعِيرِ فَقَيَّدَ بِهِ الْبَعِيرَ ثُمَّ جَاءَ يَمْشِي حَتَّى قَعَدَ مَعَنَا يَتَغَدَّى قَالَ فَنَظَرَ فِي الْقَوْمِ فَإِذَا ‏ ‏ظَهْرُهُمْ ‏ ‏فِيهِ قِلَّةٌ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ مُشَاةٌ فَلَمَّا نَظَرَ إِلَى الْقَوْمِ خَرَجَ يَعْدُو قَالَ فَأَتَى بَعِيرَهُ فَقَعَدَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ فَخَرَجَ ‏ ‏يَرْكُضُهُ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ ‏ ‏طَلِيعَةٌ ‏ ‏لِلْكُفَّارِ فَاتَّبَعَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنَّا مِنْ ‏ ‏أَسْلَمَ ‏ ‏عَلَى نَاقَةٍ لَهُ ‏ ‏وَرْقَاءَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِيَاسٌ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَبِي فَاتَّبَعْتُهُ ‏ ‏أَعْدُو عَلَى رِجْلَيَّ قَالَ وَرَأْسُ النَّاقَةِ عِنْدَ وَرِكِ الْجَمَلِ قَالَ وَلَحِقْتُهُ فَكُنْتُ عِنْدَ وَرِكِ النَّاقَةِ وَتَقَدَّمْتُ حَتَّى كُنْتُ عِنْدَ وَرِكِ الْجَمَلِ ثُمَّ تَقَدَّمْتُ حَتَّى أَخَذْتُ ‏ ‏بِخِطَامِ ‏ ‏الْجَمَلِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ إِخْ فَلَمَّا وَضْعَ الْجَمَلُ رُكْبَتَهُ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ‏ ‏اخْتَرَطْتُ ‏ ‏سَيْفِي فَضَرَبْتُ رَأْسَهُ ‏ ‏فَنَدَرَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ جِئْتُ بِرَاحِلَتِهِ أَقُودُهَا فَاسْتَقْبَلَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَعَ النَّاسِ قَالَ مَنْ قَتَلَ هَذَا الرَّجُلَ قَالُوا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَهُ ‏ ‏سَلَبُهُ ‏ ‏أَجْمَعُ ‏
en: Bahz ibn Asad narrated to us, he said: ʿIkrimah ibn ʿAmmār narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, from his father, who said: We went on the expedition against Hawāzin and Ghaṭafān with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. While we were thus, a man came on a red camel. He took something from the belly-band of the camel and hobbled it with it. Then he came walking until he sat with us to eat. He looked at the people and saw that their mounts were few and most of them were on foot. When he observed this, he went running to his camel, mounted it, and galloped away — he was a scout for the disbelievers. A man from among us, from Aslam, followed him on a gray she-camel. Iyās said: My father said: I followed him running on my two feet. The she-camel's head was at the camel's flank. I caught up and was at the she-camel's flank, then moved ahead until I was at the camel's flank, then moved ahead until I seized the camel's halter. I said to it: Ikh [kneel]! When the camel put its knee to the ground, I drew my sword and struck his head — it flew off. Then I came with his riding camel, leading it. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ met me with the people and said: "Who killed this man?" They said: Ibn al-Akwaʿ. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "All his spoils belong to him."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَعَثَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَبَا بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ ‏ ‏إِلَى ‏ ‏فَزَارَةَ ‏ ‏وَخَرَجْتُ مَعَهُ حَتَّى إِذَا ‏ ‏دَنَوْنَا ‏ ‏مِنْ الْمَاءِ ‏ ‏عَرَّسَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا صَلَّيْنَا الصُّبْحَ أَمَرَنَا ‏ ‏فَشَنَنَّا ‏ ‏الْغَارَةَ ‏ ‏فَوَرَدْنَا ‏ ‏الْمَاءَ فَقَتَلَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏مَنْ قَتَلَ وَنَحْنُ مَعَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَرَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏عُنُقًا ‏ ‏مِنْ النَّاسِ فِيهِمْ ‏ ‏الذَّرَارِيُّ ‏ ‏فَخَشِيتُ أَنْ يَسْبِقُونِي إِلَى الْجَبَلِ فَأَدْرَكْتُهُمْ فَرَمَيْتُ بِسَهْمٍ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ الْجَبَلِ فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا السَّهْمَ قَامُوا فَإِذَا امْرَأَةٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏فَزَارَةَ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهَا ‏ ‏قَشْعٌ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏أَدَمٍ ‏ ‏مَعَهَا ابْنَةٌ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ ‏ ‏الْعَرَبِ ‏ ‏فَجِئْتُ أَسُوقُهُنَّ إِلَى ‏ ‏أَبِي بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏فَنَفَّلَنِي ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏ابْنَتَهَا فَلَمْ أَكْشِفْ لَهَا ثَوْبًا حَتَّى قَدِمْتُ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ بَاتَتْ عِنْدِي فَلَمْ أَكْشِفْ لَهَا ثَوْبًا حَتَّى لَقِيَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي السُّوقِ فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏هَبْ لِي الْمَرْأَةَ قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَقَدْ أَعْجَبَتْنِي وَمَا كَشَفْتُ لَهَا ثَوْبًا قَالَ فَسَكَتَ حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ الْغَدُ لَقِيَنِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي السُّوقِ وَلَمْ أَكْشِفْ لَهَا ثَوْبًا فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏هَبْ لِي الْمَرْأَةَ لِلَّهِ أَبُوكَ قَالَ قُلْتُ هِيَ لَكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ فَبَعَثَ بِهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِلَى أَهْلِ ‏ ‏مَكَّةَ ‏ ‏فَفَدَى بِهَا أُسَرَاءَ مِنْ الْمُسْلِمِينَ كَانُوا فِي أَيْدِي الْمُشْرِكِينَ ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, he said: ʿIkrimah narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ sent Abū Bakr رضي الله عنه against Fazārah, and I went out with him. When we drew near to the water, Abū Bakr halted us for rest. When we prayed Fajr, he ordered us and we launched the raid. We reached the water, and Abū Bakr killed those he killed, with us alongside him. Salamah said: I saw a group of people, including women and children, and I feared they would reach the mountain before me. I caught up with them and shot an arrow between them and the mountain. When they saw the arrow, they stopped. There was a woman from Fazārah wearing a leather garment, and with her was a daughter who was one of the most beautiful of the Arabs. I drove them to Abū Bakr, and Abū Bakr gave me her daughter as spoils. I did not uncover a garment of hers until I reached Madīnah. She spent the night with me, and I did not uncover a garment of hers, until the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ met me in the marketplace and said: "O Salamah, give me the woman." I said: O Messenger of Allāh, she has impressed me, and I have not uncovered a garment of hers. He remained silent. The next day, the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ met me in the marketplace — and I had not uncovered a garment of hers — and said: "O Salamah, give me the woman, may Allāh bless your father." I said: She is yours, O Messenger of Allāh. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ sent her to the people of Makkah and ransomed with her Muslim captives who were in the hands of the polytheists.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَارَزَ عَمِّي يَوْمَ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏مَرْحَبٌ ‏ ‏الْيَهُودِيُّ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَرْحَبٌ ‏ ‏قَدْ عَلِمَتْ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرُ ‏ ‏أَنِّي ‏ ‏مَرْحَبُ ‏ ‏شَاكِي السِّلَاحِ بَطَلٌ مُجَرَّبُ ‏ ‏إِذَا الْحُرُوبُ أَقْبَلَتْ ‏ ‏تَلَهَّبُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ عَمِّي ‏ ‏عَامِرٌ ‏ ‏قَدْ عَلِمَتْ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرُ ‏ ‏أَنِّي ‏ ‏عَامِرُ ‏ ‏شَاكِي السِّلَاحِ بَطَلٌ ‏ ‏مُغَامِرُ ‏ ‏فَاخْتَلَفَا ضَرْبَتَيْنِ فَوَقَعَ سَيْفُ ‏ ‏مَرْحَبٍ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏تُرْسِ ‏ ‏عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏وَذَهَبَ ‏ ‏يَسْفُلُ ‏ ‏لَهُ فَرَجَعَ السَّيْفُ عَلَى سَاقِهِ قَطَعَ ‏ ‏أَكْحَلَهُ ‏ ‏فَكَانَتْ فِيهَا نَفْسُهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏لَقِيتُ نَاسًا مِنْ ‏ ‏صَحَابَةِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقَالُوا بَطَلَ عَمَلُ ‏ ‏عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ قَالَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَجِئْتُ إِلَى نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَبْكِي قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ بَطَلَ عَمَلُ ‏ ‏عَامِرٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ مَنْ قَالَ ذَاكَ قُلْتُ نَاسٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِكَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏كَذَبَ مَنْ قَالَ ذَاكَ بَلْ ‏ ‏لَهُ أَجْرُهُ مَرَّتَيْنِ إِنَّهُ حِينَ خَرَجَ إِلَى ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏جَعَلَ ‏ ‏يَرْجُزُ ‏ ‏بِأَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَفِيهِمْ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَسُوقُ ‏ ‏الرِّكَابَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ يَقُولُ ‏ ‏تَالَلَّهِ لَوْلَا اللَّهُ مَا اهْتَدَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَلَا تَصَدَّقْنَا وَلَا صَلَّيْنَا ‏ ‏إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَدْ ‏ ‏بَغَوْا ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏إِذَا أَرَادُوا ‏ ‏فِتْنَةً ‏ ‏أَبَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَنَحْنُ عَنْ فَضْلِكَ مَا اسْتَغْنَيْنَا ‏ ‏فَثَبِّتْ الْأَقْدَامَ إِنْ لَاقَيْنَا ‏ ‏وَأَنْزِلَنْ ‏ ‏سَكِينَةً ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا ‏ ‏فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ هَذَا قَالَ ‏ ‏عَامِرٌ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ غَفَرَ لَكَ رَبُّكَ قَالَ وَمَا اسْتَغْفَرَ لِإِنْسَانٍ قَطُّ يَخُصُّهُ إِلَّا اسْتُشْهِدَ فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ ذَلِكَ ‏ ‏عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَوْ ‏ ‏مَتَّعْتَنَا ‏ ‏بِعَامِرٍ فَقَدِمَ فَاسْتُشْهِدَ قَالَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ إِنَّ نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَرْسَلَنِي إِلَى ‏ ‏عَلِيٍّ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ لَأُعْطِيَنَّ ‏ ‏الرَّايَةَ ‏ ‏الْيَوْمَ رَجُلًا يُحِبُّ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ‏ ‏أَوْ يُحِبُّهُ اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَجِئْتُ بِهِ أَقُودُهُ ‏ ‏أَرْمَدَ ‏ ‏فَبَصَقَ نَبِيُّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي عَيْنِهِ ثُمَّ أَعْطَاهُ ‏ ‏الرَّايَةَ ‏ ‏فَخَرَجَ ‏ ‏مَرْحَبٌ ‏ ‏يَخْطِرُ ‏ ‏بِسَيْفِهِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏قَدْ عَلِمَتْ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرُ ‏ ‏أَنِّي ‏ ‏مَرْحَبُ ‏ ‏شَاكِي السِّلَاحِ بَطَلٌ مُجَرَّبُ ‏ ‏إِذَا الْحُرُوبُ أَقْبَلَتْ ‏ ‏تَلَهَّبُ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ‏ ‏كَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَجْهَهُ ‏ ‏أَنَا الَّذِي سَمَّتْنِي أُمِّي ‏ ‏حَيْدَرَهْ ‏ ‏كَلَيْثِ ‏ ‏غَابَاتٍ كَرِيهِ ‏ ‏الْمَنْظَرَهْ ‏ ‏أُوفِيهِمُ ‏ ‏بِالصَّاعِ ‏ ‏كَيْلَ ‏ ‏السَّنْدَرَهْ ‏ ‏فَفَلَقَ رَأْسَ ‏ ‏مَرْحَبٍ ‏ ‏بِالسَّيْفِ وَكَانَ الْفَتْحُ عَلَى يَدَيْهِ ‏
en: Abū al-Naḍr narrated to us, he said: ʿIkrimah narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah narrated to me, he said: my father informed me, saying: My uncle dueled Marḥab the Jew on the day of Khaybar. Marḥab said: "Khaybar knows that I am Marḥab, fully armed, a proven warrior — when the wars come blazing." My uncle ʿĀmir said: "Khaybar knows that I am ʿĀmir, fully armed, a daring warrior." They exchanged two blows: Marḥab's sword fell on ʿĀmir's shield, and he went to strike low, but the sword recoiled onto his own shin and severed his artery, and he died from it. Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ said: I met some of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ and they said: ʿĀmir's deeds are nullified — he killed himself. Salamah said: I came to the Prophet of Allāh ﷺ weeping and said: O Messenger of Allāh, ʿĀmir's deeds are nullified! He said: "Who said that?" I said: Some of your companions. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Whoever said that has lied. Rather, he has his reward twice. When he set out for Khaybar, he was chanting rajaz for the companions of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ — and among them was the Prophet ﷺ — driving the riding animals, saying: 'By Allāh, were it not for Allāh we would not have been guided, nor given charity, nor prayed. Those who have transgressed against us — when they intended fitnah, we refused. We are never free of need for Your bounty. So make firm our feet if we meet [the enemy], and send down tranquility upon us.'" The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Who is this?" He said: ʿĀmir, O Messenger of Allāh. He said: "May your Lord forgive you." And never did he seek forgiveness for a person specifically except that person was martyred. When ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb heard that, he said: O Messenger of Allāh, if only you had let us benefit from ʿĀmir longer. Then he went forth and was martyred. Salamah said: Then the Prophet of Allāh ﷺ sent me to ʿAlī and said: "I will surely give the banner today to a man who loves Allāh and His Messenger — or whom Allāh and His Messenger love." So I brought him, leading him while he had sore eyes. The Prophet of Allāh ﷺ spat in his eye, then gave him the banner. Marḥab came out strutting with his sword, saying: "Khaybar knows that I am Marḥab, fully armed, a proven warrior — when the wars come blazing." ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib — may Allāh honor his face — said: "I am the one my mother named Ḥaydarah [lion], like a lion of the thickets, fearsome in appearance. I repay them measure for measure, the measure of Sandarah." He split Marḥab's head with the sword, and the conquest came at his hands.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عِكْرِمَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَدِمْنَا ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏ ‏زَمَنَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ ‏ ‏مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَخَرَجْنَا أَنَا ‏ ‏وَرَبَاحٌ ‏ ‏غُلَامُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِظَهْرِ ‏ ‏رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَخَرَجْتُ بِفَرَسٍ ‏ ‏لِطَلْحَةَ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ أُرِيدُ أَنْ ‏ ‏أُبْدِيَهُ ‏ ‏مَعَ الْإِبِلِ فَلَمَّا كَانَ ‏ ‏بِغَلَسٍ ‏ ‏غَارَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏عَلَى إِبِلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَقَتَلَ رَاعِيَهَا وَخَرَجَ يَطْرُدُهَا هُوَ وَأُنَاسٌ مَعَهُ فِي خَيْلٍ فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏رَبَاحُ ‏ ‏اقْعُدْ عَلَى هَذَا الْفَرَسِ فَأَلْحِقْهُ ‏ ‏بِطَلْحَةَ ‏ ‏وَأَخْبِرْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ قَدْ أُغِيرَ عَلَى ‏ ‏سَرْحِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ وَقُمْتُ عَلَى تَلٍّ فَجَعَلْتُ وَجْهِي مِنْ قِبَلِ ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ نَادَيْتُ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏صَبَاحَاهْ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ اتَّبَعْتُ الْقَوْمَ مَعِي سَيْفِي وَنَبْلِي فَجَعَلْتُ أَرْمِيهِمْ ‏ ‏وَأَعْقِرُ ‏ ‏بِهِمْ وَذَلِكَ حِينَ يَكْثُرُ الشَّجَرُ فَإِذَا رَجَعَ إِلَيَّ فَارِسٌ جَلَسْتُ لَهُ فِي أَصْلِ شَجَرَةٍ ثُمَّ رَمَيْتُ فَلَا يُقْبِلُ عَلَيَّ فَارِسٌ إِلَّا ‏ ‏عَقَرْتُ ‏ ‏بِهِ فَجَعَلْتُ أَرْمِيهِمْ وَأَنَا أَقُولُ ‏ ‏أَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَالْيَوْمُ يَوْمُ الرُّضَّعِ ‏ ‏فَأَلْحَقُ بِرَجُلٍ مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏فَأَرْمِيهِ وَهُوَ عَلَى ‏ ‏رَاحِلَتِهِ ‏ ‏فَيَقَعُ سَهْمِي فِي الرَّحْلِ حَتَّى انْتَظَمْتُ كَتِفَهُ فَقُلْتُ خُذْهَا ‏ ‏وَأَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَالْيَوْمُ يَوْمُ الرُّضَّعِ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا كُنْتُ فِي الشَّجَرِ أَحْرَقْتُهُمْ بِالنَّبْلِ فَإِذَا تَضَايَقَتْ ‏ ‏الثَّنَايَا ‏ ‏عَلَوْتُ الْجَبَلَ ‏ ‏فَرَدَيْتُهُمْ ‏ ‏بِالْحِجَارَةِ فَمَا زَالَ ذَاكَ شَأْنِي وَشَأْنَهُمْ أَتْبَعُهُمْ ‏ ‏فَأَرْتَجِزُ ‏ ‏حَتَّى مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ شَيْئًا مِنْ ‏ ‏ظَهْرِ ‏ ‏رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِلَّا خَلَّفْتُهُ ‏ ‏وَرَاءَ ظَهْرِي فَاسْتَنْقَذْتُهُ مِنْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ثُمَّ لَمْ أَزَلْ أَرْمِيهِمْ حَتَّى أَلْقَوْا أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ثَلَاثِينَ رُمْحًا وَأَكْثَرَ مِنْ ثَلَاثِينَ ‏ ‏بُرْدَةً ‏ ‏يَسْتَخِفُّونَ مِنْهَا وَلَا يُلْقُونَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْئًا إِلَّا جَعَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ حِجَارَةً وَجَمَعْتُ عَلَى طَرِيقِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى إِذَا امْتَدَّ الضُّحَى أَتَاهُمْ ‏ ‏عُيَيْنَةُ بْنُ بَدْرٍ الْفَزَارِيُّ ‏ ‏مَدَدًا ‏ ‏لَهُمْ وَهُمْ فِي ‏ ‏ثَنِيَّةٍ ‏ ‏ضَيِّقَةٍ ثُمَّ عَلَوْتُ الْجَبَلَ فَأَنَا فَوْقَهُمْ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عُيَيْنَةُ ‏ ‏مَا هَذَا الَّذِي أَرَى قَالُوا لَقِينَا مِنْ هَذَا ‏ ‏الْبَرْحَ ‏ ‏مَا فَارَقَنَا ‏ ‏بِسَحَرٍ ‏ ‏حَتَّى الْآنَ وَأَخَذَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ فِي أَيْدِينَا وَجَعَلَهُ وَرَاءَ ظَهْرِهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏عُيَيْنَةُ ‏ ‏لَوْلَا أَنَّ هَذَا يَرَى أَنَّ وَرَاءَهُ طَلَبًا لَقَدْ تَرَكَكُمْ لِيَقُمْ إِلَيْهِ ‏ ‏نَفَرٌ ‏ ‏مِنْكُمْ فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْهُمْ أَرْبَعَةٌ فَصَعِدُوا فِي الْجَبَلِ فَلَمَّا أَسْمَعْتُهُمْ الصَّوْتَ قُلْتُ أَتَعْرِفُونِي قَالُوا وَمَنْ أَنْتَ قُلْتُ أَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَالَّذِي كَرَّمَ وَجْهَ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا يَطْلُبُنِي مِنْكُمْ رَجُلٌ فَيُدْرِكُنِي وَلَا أَطْلُبُهُ ‏ ‏فَيَفُوتُنِي ‏ ‏قَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ إِنْ أَظُنُّ قَالَ فَمَا ‏ ‏بَرِحْتُ ‏ ‏مَقْعَدِي ذَلِكَ حَتَّى نَظَرْتُ إِلَى فَوَارِسِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَتَخَلَّلُونَ الشَّجَرَ وَإِذَا أَوَّلُهُمْ ‏ ‏الْأَخْرَمُ الْأَسَدِيُّ ‏ ‏وَعَلَى ‏ ‏أَثَرِهِ ‏ ‏أَبُو قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏فَارِسُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَعَلَى أَثَرِ ‏ ‏أَبِي قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏الْمِقْدَادُ الْكِنْدِيُّ ‏ ‏فَوَلَّى الْمُشْرِكُونَ ‏ ‏مُدْبِرِينَ ‏ ‏وَأَنْزِلُ مِنْ الْجَبَلِ فَأَعْرِضُ ‏ ‏لِلْأَخْرَمِ ‏ ‏فَآخُذُ ‏ ‏بِعِنَانِ ‏ ‏فَرَسِهِ فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَخْرَمُ ‏ ‏ائْذَنْ ‏ ‏الْقَوْمَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي احْذَرْهُمْ ‏ ‏فَإِنِّي لَا آمَنُ أَنْ يَقْطَعُوكَ ‏ ‏فَاتَّئِدْ ‏ ‏حَتَّى يَلْحَقَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَصْحَابُهُ قَالَ يَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏إِنْ كُنْتَ تُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَتَعْلَمُ أَنَّ الْجَنَّةَ حَقٌّ وَالنَّارَ حَقٌّ فَلَا تَحُلْ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ الشَّهَادَةِ قَالَ فَخَلَّيْتُ ‏ ‏عِنَانَ ‏ ‏فَرَسِهِ فَيَلْحَقُ ‏ ‏بِعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ ‏ ‏وَيَعْطِفُ عَلَيْهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏فَاخْتَلَفَا ‏ ‏طَعْنَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏فَعَقَرَ ‏ ‏الْأَخْرَمُ ‏ ‏بِعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏وَطَعَنَهُ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏فَقَتَلَهُ فَتَحَوَّلَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ فَرَسِ ‏ ‏الْأَخْرَمِ ‏ ‏فَيَلْحَقُ ‏ ‏أَبُو قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏بِعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏فَاخْتَلَفَا طَعْنَتَيْنِ ‏ ‏فَعَقَرَ ‏ ‏بِأَبِي قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏وَقَتَلَهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏وَتَحَوَّلَ ‏ ‏أَبُو قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏عَلَى فَرَسِ ‏ ‏الْأَخْرَمِ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ إِنِّي خَرَجْتُ ‏ ‏أَعْدُو ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏أَثَرِ ‏ ‏الْقَوْمِ حَتَّى مَا أَرَى مِنْ غُبَارِ ‏ ‏صَحَابَةِ النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏شَيْئًا ‏ ‏وَيُعْرِضُونَ ‏ ‏قَبْلَ غَيْبُوبَةِ الشَّمْسِ إِلَى ‏ ‏شِعْبٍ ‏ ‏فِيهِ مَاءٌ يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏ذُو قَرَدٍ ‏ ‏فَأَرَادُوا أَنْ يَشْرَبُوا مِنْهُ فَأَبْصَرُونِي ‏ ‏أَعْدُو ‏ ‏وَرَاءَهُمْ ‏ ‏فَعَطَفُوا ‏ ‏عَنْهُ وَاشْتَدُّوا فِي ‏ ‏الثَّنِيَّةِ ‏ ‏ثَنِيَّةِ ذِي بِئْرٍ ‏ ‏وَغَرَبَتْ الشَّمْسُ فَأَلْحَقُ رَجُلًا ‏ ‏فَأَرْمِيهِ فَقُلْتُ خُذْهَا ‏ ‏وَأَنَا ‏ ‏ابْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَالْيَوْمُ يَوْمُ الرُّضَّعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏يَا ثُكْلَ ‏ ‏أُمِّ أَكْوَعَ ‏ ‏بَكْرَةً ‏ ‏قُلْتُ نَعَمْ أَيْ عَدُوَّ نَفْسِهِ وَكَانَ الَّذِي رَمَيْتُهُ ‏ ‏بَكْرَةً ‏ ‏فَأَتْبَعْتُهُ سَهْمًا آخَرَ فَعَلِقَ بِهِ سَهْمَانِ وَيَخْلُفُونَ فَرَسَيْنِ فَجِئْتُ بِهِمَا أَسُوقُهُمَا إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَهُوَ عَلَى الْمَاءِ الَّذِي ‏ ‏جَلَّيْتُهُمْ ‏ ‏عَنْهُ ‏ ‏ذُوْ قَرَدٍ ‏ ‏فَإِذَا بِنَبِيِّ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي خَمْسِ مِائَةٍ وَإِذَا ‏ ‏بِلَالٌ ‏ ‏قَدْ ‏ ‏نَحَرَ ‏ ‏جَزُورًا ‏ ‏مِمَّا خَلَّفْتُ فَهُوَ ‏ ‏يَشْوِي لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مِنْ كَبِدِهَا ‏ ‏وَسَنَامِهَا ‏ ‏فَأَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ خَلِّنِي ‏ ‏فَأَنْتَخِبَ ‏ ‏مِنْ أَصْحَابِكَ مِائَةً فَآخُذَ عَلَى الْكُفَّارِ ‏ ‏عَشْوَةً ‏ ‏فَلَا يَبْقَى مِنْهُمْ ‏ ‏مُخْبِرٌ ‏ ‏إِلَّا قَتَلْتُهُ قَالَ أَكُنْتَ فَاعِلًا ذَلِكَ يَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏قَالَ نَعَمْ وَالَّذِي أَكْرَمَكَ فَضَحِكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏حَتَّى رَأَيْتُ ‏ ‏نَوَاجِذَهُ ‏ ‏فِي ضُوءِ النَّارِ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّهُمْ ‏ ‏يُقْرَوْنَ ‏ ‏الْآنَ بِأَرْضِ ‏ ‏غَطَفَانَ ‏ ‏فَجَاءَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏غَطَفَانَ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ مَرُّوا عَلَى فُلَانٍ ‏ ‏الْغَطَفَانِيِّ ‏ ‏فَنَحَرَ ‏ ‏لَهُمْ ‏ ‏جَزُورًا ‏ ‏قَالَ فَلَمَّا أَخَذُوا يَكْشِطُونَ جِلْدَهَا رَأَوْا غَبَرَةً فَتَرَكُوهَا وَخَرَجُوا هَرَبًا فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحْنَا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏خَيْرُ فُرْسَانِنَا الْيَوْمَ ‏ ‏أَبُو قَتَادَةَ ‏ ‏وَخَيْرُ رَجَّالَتِنَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَأَعْطَانِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏سَهْمَ ‏ ‏الرَّاجِلِ ‏ ‏وَالْفَارِسِ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ ‏ ‏أَرْدَفَنِي ‏ ‏وَرَاءَهُ عَلَى ‏ ‏الْعَضْبَاءِ ‏ ‏رَاجِعِينَ إِلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏فَلَمَّا كَانَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهَا قَرِيبًا مِنْ ‏ ‏ضَحْوَةٍ ‏ ‏وَفِي الْقَوْمِ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏كَانَ لَا يُسْبَقُ جَعَلَ يُنَادِي هَلْ مِنْ مُسَابِقٍ أَلَا رَجُلٌ يُسَابِقُ إِلَى ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةِ ‏ ‏فَأَعَادَ ذَلِكَ مِرَارًا وَأَنَا وَرَاءَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مُرْدِفِي ‏ ‏قُلْتُ لَهُ أَمَا تُكْرِمُ كَرِيمًا وَلَا تَهَابُ شَرِيفًا قَالَ لَا إِلَّا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَأُمِّي خَلِّنِي فَلَأُسَابِقُ الرَّجُلَ قَالَ إِنْ شِئْتَ قُلْتُ أَذْهَبُ إِلَيْكَ ‏ ‏فَطَفَرَ ‏ ‏عَنْ رَاحِلَتِهِ وَثَنَيْتُ رِجْلَيَّ ‏ ‏فَطَفَرْتُ ‏ ‏عَنْ النَّاقَةِ ثُمَّ إِنِّي رَبَطْتُ عَلَيْهَا ‏ ‏شَرَفًا ‏ ‏أَوْ ‏ ‏شَرَفَيْنِ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي اسْتَبْقَيْتُ نَفْسِي ‏ ‏ثُمَّ إِنِّي عَدَوْتُ حَتَّى أَلْحَقَهُ ‏ ‏فَأَصُكَّ ‏ ‏بَيْنَ كَتِفَيْهِ بِيَدَيَّ قُلْتُ سَبَقْتُكَ وَاللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَوْ كَلِمَةً نَحْوَهَا ‏ ‏قَالَ فَضَحِكَ وَقَالَ إِنْ أَظُنُّ حَتَّى قَدِمْنَا ‏ ‏الْمَدِينَةَ ‏
en: Hāshim ibn al-Qāsim narrated to us, ʿIkrimah ibn ʿAmmār narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, from his father, who said: We arrived in Madīnah at the time of al-Ḥudaybiyyah with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. I went out with Rabāḥ, the servant of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, with the riding animals of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. I also took out a horse belonging to Ṭalḥah ibn ʿUbaydillāh, intending to graze it with the camels. At dawn, ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿUyaynah raided the camels of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, killed their herdsman, and drove them away with men on horseback. I said: O Rabāḥ, get on this horse and deliver it to Ṭalḥah, and inform the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ that his livestock has been raided. I stood on a hill, faced Madīnah, and cried out three times: "O the morning raid!" Then I followed the raiders with my sword and arrows, shooting at them and hamstringing their mounts. Whenever a horseman turned back toward me, I sat at the base of a tree and shot him — every horseman who came at me, I hamstrung his mount. I kept shooting them, saying: "I am Ibn al-Akwaʿ, and today is the day of the sucklings!" I would catch up to one of them on his camel and shoot him, my arrow landing in the saddle until it pierced his shoulder. I said: "Take that! I am Ibn al-Akwaʿ, and today is the day of the sucklings!" When among trees, I burned them with arrows; when the passes narrowed, I climbed the mountain and pelted them with stones. This continued — I pursuing them and chanting rajaz — until I had rescued every last camel of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, placing them behind me. I kept shooting them until they dropped more than thirty spears and more than thirty cloaks to lighten their load. Everything they dropped, I piled stones on it and gathered it on the path of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. When mid-morning came, ʿUyaynah ibn Badr al-Fazārī came as reinforcement in a narrow pass. I climbed the mountain above them. ʿUyaynah said: What is this I see? They said: We have met severe trouble from this man — he has not left us since dawn until now, and he has taken everything from our hands and placed it behind him. ʿUyaynah said: Were it not that he thinks there is pursuit behind him, he would have left you. Let some of you go up to him. Four of them climbed the mountain. When I made them hear my voice, I said: Do you know me? They said: Who are you? I said: I am Ibn al-Akwaʿ. By the One who honored the face of Muḥammad ﷺ, no man among you can pursue me and catch me, nor can I pursue him and he escape me. One of them said: I think so too. I did not leave my position until I saw the horsemen of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ threading through the trees. The first of them was al-Akhram al-Asadī, and behind him Abū Qatādah, the horseman of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, and behind Abū Qatādah was al-Miqdād al-Kindī. The polytheists turned and fled. I came down from the mountain and intercepted al-Akhram, seizing his horse's reins. I said: O Akhram, beware of the enemy — I fear they will cut you off. Wait until the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and his companions catch up. He said: O Salamah, if you believe in Allāh and the Last Day, and you know that Paradise is real and the Fire is real, do not stand between me and martyrdom. So I released his horse's reins. He caught up with ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿUyaynah, and ʿAbd al-Raḥmān turned on him. They exchanged two thrusts: al-Akhram hamstrung ʿAbd al-Raḥmān's mount, and ʿAbd al-Raḥmān speared and killed him. ʿAbd al-Raḥmān switched to al-Akhram's horse. Then Abū Qatādah caught up with ʿAbd al-Raḥmān — they exchanged two thrusts: he hamstrung Abū Qatādah's mount, and Abū Qatādah killed him. Abū Qatādah switched to al-Akhram's horse. Then I went running after the enemy until I could no longer see the dust of the Prophet's ﷺ companions. They turned before sunset toward a valley with water called Dhū Qarad. They wanted to drink, but they saw me running behind them, so they veered away and raced through the pass of Dhī Biʾr. The sun set. I caught up to a man among them and shot him, saying: "Take that! I am Ibn al-Akwaʿ, and today is the day of the sucklings!" He said: Woe to his mother — Akwaʿ so early? I said: Yes, O enemy of yourself! He was the one I had shot earlier. I followed up with another arrow — two arrows stuck in him. They left behind two horses, which I drove to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ at the water I had driven them from — Dhū Qarad. There was the Prophet of Allāh ﷺ with five hundred men, and Bilāl had slaughtered a camel from those I had rescued and was roasting its liver and hump for the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. I came to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ and said: O Messenger of Allāh, let me select a hundred of your companions and take the disbelievers by a night raid — I will not leave any of them alive to report it. He said: "Would you really do that, O Salamah?" I said: Yes, by the One who honored you. The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ laughed until I saw his molars in the firelight. Then he said: "They are being hosted now in the land of Ghaṭafān." A man from Ghaṭafān came and said: They stopped at so-and-so the Ghaṭafānī, and he slaughtered a camel for them. When they started skinning it, they saw a dust cloud, so they abandoned it and fled. The next morning, the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "The best of our horsemen today is Abū Qatādah, and the best of our foot soldiers is Salamah." The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ gave me the share of both the foot soldier and the horseman combined. Then he seated me behind him on al-ʿAḍbāʾ [his she-camel] returning to Madīnah. When we were about a morning's journey away, there was a man from the Anṣār in the group who was never outrun. He kept calling: Is there a racer? Will no one race to Madīnah? He repeated this several times. I was behind the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ riding double. I said to him: Do you not honor a noble man or respect a distinguished one? He said: No — except the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. I said: O Messenger of Allāh, may my father and mother be your ransom — let me race the man. He said: "If you wish." I said: I'll come to you [at the finish]. He dismounted from his riding camel, and I tucked my legs and leapt off the she-camel. Then I paced myself for a rise or two — meaning I saved my energy — then I sprinted until I caught up with him and slapped him between the shoulders with my hand. I said: I beat you, by Allāh — or words to that effect. He laughed and said: I think so too. And we arrived in Madīnah.

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ بْنُ عُتْبَةَ أَبُو يَحْيَى ‏ ‏قَاضِي ‏ ‏الْيَمَامَةِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سَمِعْتُ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقُولُ ‏ ‏إِذَا حَضَرَتْ الصَّلَاةُ وَالْعَشَاءُ فَابْدَءُوا بِالْعَشَاءِ ‏
en: Abū al-Naḍr narrated to us, he said: Ayyūb ibn ʿUtbah Abū Yaḥyā, the judge of al-Yamāmah, narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ narrated to us, from his father, who said: I heard the Prophet ﷺ say: "When the prayer and the evening meal are both present, begin with the meal."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو النَّضْرِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَيُّوبُ بْنُ عُتْبَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏مَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَّ ‏ ‏عَلَيْنَا السَّيْفَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا ‏
en: Abū al-Naḍr narrated to us, he said: Ayyūb ibn ʿUtbah narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah narrated to us, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ said: "Whoever unsheathes the sword against us is not one of us."

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏كَانَ ‏ ‏يَتَحَرَّى ‏ ‏مَوْضِعَ الْمُصْحَفِ وَذَكَرَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَتَحَرَّى ‏ ‏ذَلِكَ الْمَكَانَ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ بَيْنَ الْمِنْبَرِ وَالْقِبْلَةِ مَمَرُّ شَاةٍ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd, from Salamah, that he used to deliberately choose the spot near the Muṣḥaf [to pray]. He mentioned that the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ used to deliberately choose that spot. Between the minbar and the qiblah there was room for a sheep to pass.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏غَزَوْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏سَبْعَ غَزَوَاتٍ فَذَكَرَ ‏ ‏الْحُدَيْبِيَةَ ‏ ‏وَيَوْمَ ‏ ‏حُنَيْنٍ ‏ ‏وَيَوْمَ ‏ ‏الْقَرَدِ ‏ ‏وَيَوْمَ ‏ ‏خَيْبَرَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ ‏ ‏وَنَسِيتُ بَقِيَّتَهُنَّ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd, from Salamah, who said: I went on seven expeditions with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. He mentioned al-Ḥudaybiyyah, the day of Ḥunayn, the day of al-Qarad, and the day of Khaybar. Yazīd said: I forgot the rest of them.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَنِي عَمِّي ‏ ‏عَامِرٌ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ أَعْطِنِي سِلَاحَكَ قَالَ فَأَعْطَيْتُهُ قَالَ فَجِئْتُ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَبْغِنِي سِلَاحَكَ قَالَ أَيْنَ سِلَاحُكَ قَالَ قُلْتُ أَعْطَيْتُهُ عَمِّي ‏ ‏عَامِرًا ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا أَجِدُ شَبَهَكَ إِلَّا الَّذِي قَالَ هَبْ لِي أَخًا أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ نَفْسِي قَالَ فَأَعْطَانِي قَوْسَهُ ‏ ‏وَمَجَانَّهُ ‏ ‏وَثَلَاثَةَ أَسْهُمٍ مِنْ ‏ ‏كِنَانَتِهِ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd — meaning Ibn Abī ʿUbayd — from Salamah, who said: My uncle ʿĀmir came to me and said: Give me your weapons. So I gave them to him. Then I went to the Prophet ﷺ and said: O Messenger of Allāh, equip me with weapons. He said: "Where are your weapons?" I said: I gave them to my uncle ʿĀmir. He said: "I find no comparison for you except the one who said: 'Grant me a brother dearer to me than myself.'" Then he gave me his bow, his shield, and three arrows from his quiver.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏يَزِيدَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ اسْتَأْذَنَ النَّبِيَّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏الْبَدْوِ ‏ ‏فَأَذِنَ لَهُ ‏
en: Ḥammād ibn Masʿadah narrated to us, from Yazīd, from Salamah, that he asked the Prophet ﷺ for permission to live in the desert, and he granted him permission.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو سَلَمَةَ الْخُزَاعِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى بْنُ الْحَارِثِ ‏ ‏قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَأَبُو أَحْمَدَ الزُّبَيْرِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَعْلَى ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا ‏ ‏نُصَلِّي مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏الْجُمُعَةَ ثُمَّ نَرْجِعُ وَمَا ‏ ‏لِلْحِيطَانِ ‏ ‏فَيْءٌ ‏ ‏يُسْتَظَلُّ بِهِ ‏
en: Abū Salamah al-Khuzāʿī narrated to us, he said: Yaʿlā ibn al-Ḥārith informed us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ informed us. And Abū Aḥmad al-Zubayrī, he said: Yaʿlā narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah narrated to me, from his father, who said: We used to pray the Friday prayer with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, then we would return and the walls had no shade to take shelter in.

ar: ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عِيسَى ‏ ‏وَيُونُسُ ‏ ‏وَهَذَا حَدِيثُ ‏ ‏إِسْحَاقَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَطَّافُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ الْمَخْزُومِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏مُوسَى بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏ابْنُ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ سَمِعْتُ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏وَكَانَ إِذَا نَزَلَ يَنْزِلُ عَلَى أَبِي ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏إِنِّي أَكُونُ فِي الصَّيْدِ وَلَيْسَ عَلَيَّ إِلَّا قَمِيصٌ ‏ ‏أَفَأُصَلِّي فِيهِ قَالَ ‏ ‏زُرَّهُ ‏ ‏وَلَوْ لَمْ تَجِدْ إِلَّا شَوْكَةً ‏
en: Isḥāq ibn ʿĪsā and Yūnus — and this is the ḥadīth of Isḥāq — he said: ʿAṭṭāf ibn Khālid al-Makhzūmī narrated to us, he said: Mūsā ibn Ibrāhīm narrated to me — Yūnus said: Ibn Abī Rabīʿah — he said: I heard Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ — and when he came down [to visit], he would stay at my father's house — he said: I said: O Messenger of Allāh, I am sometimes out hunting with nothing on but a shirt. May I pray in it? He said: "Button it up, even if only with a thorn."

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عُمَرُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ الْيَمَامِيُّ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏إِيَاسُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ الْأَسْلَمِيُّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏مَا سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَسْتَفْتِحُ دُعَاءً إِلَّا اسْتَفْتَحَهُ بِسُبْحَانَ ‏ ‏رَبِّيَ الْأَعْلَى الْعَلِيِّ الْوَهَّابِ ‏
en: ʿAbd al-Ṣamad narrated to us, he said: ʿUmar ibn Rāshid al-Yamāmī narrated to us, he said: Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ al-Aslamī narrated to us, from his father, who said: I never heard the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ begin a supplication except that he began it with: "Subḥāna Rabbī al-Aʿlā al-Wahhāb" [Glory be to my Lord, the Most High, the Bestower].

ar: ‏ ‏وَقَالَ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِيمَنْ ‏ ‏بَايَعَهُ ‏ ‏تَحْتَ الشَّجَرَةِ ثُمَّ مَرَرْتُ بِهِ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ وَمَعَهُ قَوْمٌ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏بَايِعْ ‏ ‏يَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ قَدْ فَعَلْتُ قَالَ وَأَيْضًا ‏ ‏فَبَايَعْتُهُ ‏ ‏الثَّانِيَةَ ‏
en: Salamah said: I pledged allegiance to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ among those who pledged under the tree. Then I passed by him after that while he was with some people, and he said: "Pledge, O Salamah." I said: I have already done so. He said: "And again." So I pledged to him a second time.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَكِّيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُ ‏ ‏رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ عَدَلْتُ إِلَى ظِلِّ شَجَرَةٍ فَلَمَّا خَفَّ النَّاسُ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ يَا ‏ ‏ابْنَ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏أَلَا ‏ ‏تُبَايِعُ ‏ ‏قُلْتُ قَدْ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُ ‏ ‏يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ قَالَ وَأَيْضًا قَالَ ‏ ‏فَبَايَعْتُهُ ‏ ‏الثَّانِيَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ ‏ ‏فَقُلْتُ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا مُسْلِمٍ ‏ ‏عَلَى أَيِّ شَيْءٍ ‏ ‏تُبَايِعُونَ ‏ ‏يَوْمَئِذٍ قَالَ ‏ ‏عَلَى الْمَوْتِ ‏
en: Makkī ibn Ibrāhīm narrated to us, he said: Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: I pledged allegiance to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, then I turned aside to the shade of a tree. When the crowd thinned around the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ, he said: "O Ibn al-Akwaʿ, will you not pledge?" I said: I have already pledged, O Messenger of Allāh. He said: "And again." So I pledged to him a second time. Yazīd said: I asked him: O Abū Muslim, upon what did you pledge that day? He said: Upon death.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏مَكِّيٌّ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَزِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُبَيْدٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنَّا ‏ ‏نُصَلِّي الْمَغْرِبَ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏إِذَا ‏ ‏تَوَارَتْ ‏ ‏بِالْحِجَابِ ‏
en: Makkī narrated to us, Yazīd ibn Abī ʿUbayd narrated to us, from Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, who said: We used to pray Maghrib with the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ when the sun disappeared behind the horizon.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْعَطَّافُ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏وَقَالَ غَيْرُ ‏ ‏يُونُسَ بْنِ رَزِينٍ ‏ ‏أَنَّهُ ‏ ‏نَزَلَ ‏ ‏الرَّبَذَةَ ‏ ‏هُوَ وَأَصْحَابُهُ يُرِيدُونَ الْحَجَّ قِيلَ لَهُمْ هَاهُنَا ‏ ‏سَلَمَةُ بْنُ الْأَكْوَعِ صَاحِبُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَأَتَيْنَاهُ فَسَلَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ سَأَلْنَاهُ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏بَايَعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِيَدِي هَذِهِ وَأَخْرَجَ لَنَا كَفَّهُ كَفًّا ضَخْمَةً قَالَ فَقُمْنَا إِلَيْهِ فَقَبَّلْنَا ‏ ‏كَفَّيْهِ جَمِيعًا ‏
en: Yūnus narrated to us, he said: al-ʿAṭṭāf narrated to us, he said: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān narrated to me — my father said: and others besides Yūnus [say] Ibn Razīn — that he stopped at al-Rabadhah, he and his companions intending Ḥajj. It was said to them: Here is Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, the companion of the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ. So we went to him and greeted him, then asked him. He said: I pledged allegiance to the Messenger of Allāh ﷺ with this hand of mine — and he showed us his palm, a large palm. We stood up and kissed both his palms.

ar: ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يُونُسُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَبْدُ الْوَاحِدِ بْنُ زِيَادٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو عُمَيْسٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِيَاسِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِيهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏رَخَّصَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فِي ‏ ‏مُتْعَةِ النِّسَاءِ ‏ ‏عَامَ ‏ ‏أَوْطَاسٍ ‏ ‏ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ نَهَى عَنْهَا ‏
en: Yūnus ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, he said: ʿAbd al-Wāḥid ibn Ziyād narrated to us, he said: Abū ʿUmays narrated to us, from Iyās ibn Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allāh ﷺ granted a concession for the temporary marriage of women in the year of Awṭās for three days, then he forbade it.
